title: Recursion
summary: Packages finite-order Ricci recursion data and the first-jet relation with the Schouten tensor.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Packages finite-order Ricci recursion data and the first-jet relation with the Schouten tensor.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows Ambient/FG/Recursion.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A solved finite FG Ricci recursion for a formal metric jet. Its mathematical data are: solved Coeff: m : Fin K plus 1 maps to Sym2 Component ι; solved Coeff eq: for every m : Fin K plus 1 i j : ι, solved Coeff m i j equals J.coeff m i j.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \begin{aligned}\operatorname{solvedCoeff}&\colon (m \colon \operatorname{Fin} (K + 1)) \longrightarrow \operatorname{Sym2Component} ι\\\operatorname{solvedCoeff\_eq}&\colon \forall (m \colon \operatorname{Fin} (K + 1)) (i j \colon ι), \operatorname{solvedCoeff} m i j = J.\operatorname{coeff} m i j\end{aligned}

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Access the solved coefficient at order m <= K. In explicit terms, for r of type formal ricci recursion ι : equals ι j, m in the natural numbers, and hm of type m is at most k, its defining expression is R.solved Coeff ⟨m, Nat.lt succ of le hm⟩.
correspondence.leanType: def solvedAt (R : FormalRicciRecursion (ι := ι) J) (m : ℕ) (hm : m ≤ K) : Sym2Component ι

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For R of type Formal Ricci Recursion ι : equals ι J, R.solved At 0 Nat.zero le K equals J.base Metric.
correspondence.leanType: theorem solvedAt_zero (R : FormalRicciRecursion (ι := ι) J) : R.solvedAt 0 (Nat.zero_le K) = J.baseMetric

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The solved first coefficient is 2 * Schouten under the first Ricci condition. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,R\colon \operatorname{FormalRicciRecursion} (ι = ι) J,\;H\colon \operatorname{SatisfiesFGRicciToOrder} J 1,\;i,j\colon ι,\quad R.\operatorname{solvedAt} 1 H.\operatorname{order\_le} i j = 2 \cdot J.\operatorname{schouten} i j
correspondence.leanType: theorem solved_firstJet_eq_two_schouten (R : FormalRicciRecursion (ι := ι) J) (H : SatisfiesFGRicciToOrder J 1) (i j : ι) : R.solvedAt 1 H.order_le i j = 2 * J.schouten i j

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Data asserting that the FG Ricci equations have been solved recursively to a finite order. Its mathematical data are: conditions: Satisfies FGRicci To Order J order; recursion: Formal Ricci Recursion J.
correspondence.leanType: structure FGRicciSolvedToOrder (J : FormalMetricJet ι K) (order : ℕ)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The first solved coefficient of a finite FG solution is 2 * Schouten. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,S\colon \operatorname{FGRicciSolvedToOrder} (ι = ι) J 1,\;i,j\colon ι,\quad S.\operatorname{recursion}.\operatorname{solvedAt} 1 S.\operatorname{conditions}.\operatorname{order\_le} i j = 2 \cdot J.\operatorname{schouten} i j
correspondence.leanType: theorem FGRicciSolvedToOrder.firstJet (S : FGRicciSolvedToOrder (ι := ι) J 1) (i j : ι) : S.recursion.solvedAt 1 S.conditions.order_le i j = 2 * J.schouten i j

references.locator: Chapters 2–3, ambient space and normal form
references.note: Geometric source for the ambient bundle, homogeneity, normal form, and commutator calculations.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
